Trump Friend Asked ICE to Detain the Mother of His Child
Last year, Paolo Zampolli, the former modeling agent who famously introduced Donald and Melania Trump, asked the administration for a favor.
Zampolli was in a custody battle with his Brazilian ex-girlfriend over their son. When she got arrested, on charges of fraud at her workplace, Zampolli saw an opportunity to get his son back.
We found that he reached out to an ICE official and asked for his ex to be detained. The official sprang into action. The woman was taken into ICE custody and ultimately deported.
Zampolli said he merely asked the ICE official what was going on with his ex’s case. The administration said she was not detained or removed because of politics or favors.
Our reporting reflects a recurring theme of the second Trump administration: The levers of the federal government can be pulled to settle a personal score.
